rounds.description: 转折 / 并列 / 因果 / 对比 / 让步 / 总结

tokens.v: Both Claire and Paul catch part of the truth,
their "free or not" way of framing the question is too sharp. I would argue against full free college for everyone,
instead support a more targeted plan: free tuition for low-income students,

income-based loans for everyone else.
Claire is right that access matters.
tuition is free for everyone, families who could easily pay also get the benefit.
Paul worries, enrollment goes up

the number of classrooms does not.
A better plan targets the real problem. Students from low-income families pay nothing,
removes the real barrier. Other students take loans
only start paying back

their income passes a set level.
Fairness is kept,
students still feel the value of what they are paying for.

rounds.name: Round 4 · 句法骨架
rounds.description: 整块从句 / 介词短语 / 修饰结构

tokens.v: Both Claire and Paul catch part of the truth,

tokens.answer: but their "free or not" way of framing the question is too sharp.

tokens.v: I would argue against full free college for everyone, and instead support

tokens.answer: a more targeted plan: free tuition for low-income students, plus income-based loans for everyone else.

tokens.v: Claire is right that access matters. But

tokens.answer: when tuition is free for everyone, families who could easily pay also get the benefit.

tokens.v: In Germany, wealthy parents get the same subsidy as poor ones, and

tokens.answer: public money that could have helped needier students gets spread thin across the whole population.

tokens.v: At the same time, as Paul worries,

tokens.answer: enrollment goes up while the number of classrooms does not.

tokens.v:
Crowded lectures and falling quality have already shown up in several European countries.
A better plan targets the real problem. Students from low-income families pay nothing,
Other students take loans and

tokens.answer: only start paying back after their income passes a set level —

tokens.v: this is the model Australia has used for years. Fairness is kept, wealthy families stop free-riding, and

tokens.answer: students still feel the value of what they are paying for.
